title: Evening By Qianmen
subtitle: Qianmen / Dashilan
blurb: Walk Qianmen and Dashilan first, then choose dinner without spending the evening in a random queue.
mood: Signs, queues, and warm pre-dinner noise

microcopy.line: Look at menus and queues first, then sit down for dinner.

content.intro:
Start the evening on Qianmen, turn into Dashilan, and do not sit in the first restaurant just because the sign is bright. Make one loop, look at menus, queues, and seating, then choose duck or another dinner.

After eating, keep the plan flexible: if you have energy and the program works, go to Lao She Teahouse; if not, walk to Sanlihe or head straight to the metro. Shops and restaurants here change, so check hours and recent reviews on the day.
content.routeLogic:
On Qianmen, look at facades and side entrances into Dashilan.
In Dashilan, pass through the crowd without stopping at every window.
Before dinner, compare menus and queues in two or three places.
Add Lao She Teahouse only after checking the program.
Use Sanlihe as a short water-side exit after eating.
content.bestFor:
an evening after a museum when you do not want a long transfer
a first dinner in the city with time to choose
old signs, lively streets, and a short walk after food
content.notFor:
travelers who want a quiet dinner
crowd fatigue
when you need to reach a show without checking the schedule
content.beforeYouGo:
Arrive before the dinner peak so the restaurant choice is not made in the densest queue.
For old Beijing brands, check the exact address because branches can differ.
Check the Lao She program on the day, usually through WeChat or an official channel.
content.timing: Start 1.5-2 hours before dinner. Walk Qianmen and Dashilan first, then sit down to eat; add the teahouse after dinner only if schedule and energy match.
content.fallbackPlan: If queues are too long, move into nearby lanes or switch from duck to noodles, snacks, and tea. If rain starts, keep Qianmen, dinner, and a taxi back.

stopList.name: Qianmen Street
stopList.note: Look around before turning into Dashilan

content.context: On Qianmen, walk the broad street south of the square and look at facades, signs, and side entrances into Dashilan. Do not spend all your appetite and time here; it is a good warm-up before denser streets.
content.lookFor:
View toward Qianmen Gate
Old shop signs
Side entrances into Dashilan
content.practical:
Evenings are busier.
Compare menus before sitting down.
Do not spend your whole appetite on first snacks.
content.nextMove: Turn into Dashilan, where the streets become narrower and denser.

stopList.name: Dashilan
stopList.note: Notice the signs but skip weak stops

content.context: In Dashilan, stay on the main street only while it stays interesting: old lettering, narrow facades, and queues at well-known places. If the windows start to repeat or the crowd stalls, turn into a side street and return to choosing dinner.
content.lookFor:
Old lettering on signs
Narrow facades
Queues at popular places
content.practical:
A queue does not always mean the best choice.
Check recent reviews for the exact address.
Side streets are easier for a short breather.
content.nextMove: After a loop through Dashilan, choose where to eat: here or just off the main flow.

stopList.name: Old Brands
stopList.note: Check the exact address, not only the name

content.context: At old Beijing brands, first check the address and watch what people ahead of you buy. A famous name cannot rescue poor seating or a tired kitchen, so do not buy a large set before the main dinner.
content.lookFor:
Signs with dates
Open kitchens
What local visitors are buying
content.practical:
Check the address because brands can have different branches.
Do not order a large set before dinner.
If the queue is not moving, change the plan.
content.nextMove: Move on to Peking duck dinner or choose a simpler nearby meal.

stopList.name: Duck Dinner
stopList.note: Sit before peak time or keep a backup dinner

content.context: If you want Peking duck, sit down before the peak or book ahead. At rush time, do not chase the loudest sign. Choose a place where you can be seated properly and will not spend the whole evening waiting.
content.lookFor:
Seating queue
How the duck is carved
Sauces and pancakes
content.practical:
Check the portion size.
For two people, half a duck and a couple of dishes can be enough.
If booking fails, do not wait at any cost.
content.nextMove: After dinner, choose between a tea performance, a short walk, or going straight to the metro.

stopList.name: Lao She Teahouse
stopList.note: Go only if schedule and energy line up

content.context: Go to Lao She Teahouse only after checking the program and start time. After a heavy dinner, a show can turn into one obligation too many, so keep it as an evening bonus rather than a fixed stop.
content.lookFor:
Current program
Program length
Available seats after dinner
content.practical:
Check the schedule on the day.
The WeChat channel is often more useful than old pages.
Do not place the show tight against a late restaurant booking.
content.nextMove: If the program does not work, walk to Sanlihe or finish the evening at the metro.
content.sourceNote: Show schedules, sales channels, and start times change; check the current program.

stopList.name: Sanlihe Park
stopList.note: A short water-side exit after dinner

content.context: After dinner, walk to Sanlihe if you want movement without more shop windows. Water, paths, and lighting make a short calm exit from the noise. If rain or fatigue is already too much, replace the park with a taxi.
content.lookFor:
Canals and small bridges
Evening lighting
Quieter passages
content.practical:
Paths can be slippery after rain.
Late in the evening, stay near a clear exit.
If you are tired, choose a taxi instead.
content.nextMove: Head to the nearest metro or call DiDi back to the hotel.
